meta.description: Canonical Canadian cannabis constitutional case law referenced in CCLDR educational content
meta.disclaimer: Educational purposes only. Not legal advice.

cases.significance: BENO-X Pillar 1 — Struck down the absolute prohibition on cannabis possession; held that denying a seriously ill person access to a medically necessary substance violated s.7 of the Charter (right to life, liberty, security). Court gave Parliament 12 months to fix the CDSA.
cases.outcome: Crown lost. Prohibition unconstitutional as applied to medical users.

cases.significance: BENO-X Pillar 2 — Held that the MMAR (Medical Marihuana Access Regulations) supply provisions were unconstitutional because they did not provide a practical, legal supply chain for authorized patients. A right without practical access is no right at all.
cases.outcome: MMAR partially struck down. Supply access confirmed as constitutional requirement.

cases.significance: BENO-X Pillar 3 — SCC unanimously struck down the restriction of medical cannabis authorization to dried plant form only. Patients have a constitutional right to access non-smokable forms (oils, edibles, etc.). Restriction was arbitrary and overbroad.
cases.outcome: Restriction on form of medical cannabis unconstitutional. Crown lost unanimously.

cases.significance: BENO-X Pillar 4 — Federal Court held that MMPR (Marihuana for Medical Purposes Regulations) violated s.7 by eliminating personal production and forcing patients to purchase from licensed producers at prices they often could not afford.
cases.outcome: MMPR personal production prohibition struck down. ACMPR introduced as replacement.

cases.significance: BENO-X Pillar 5 — Ongoing constitutional challenges to current ACMPR provisions; continuing evolution of access rights jurisprudence.

cases.citation: Stewart v Elk Valley Coal Corp
cases.significance: SCC upheld employer's right to require disclosure of addictions/substance dependencies before a safety incident in safety-sensitive workplaces. Relevant to employment law and cannabis use disclosure obligations.
cases.outcome: Employer policy upheld. Disclosure obligations in safety-sensitive roles enforceable.

charter_sections.s.7: Right to life, liberty, and security of the person
charter_sections.s.8: Right to be secure against unreasonable search and seizure
charter_sections.s.9: Right not to be arbitrarily detained or imprisoned
charter_sections.s.10b: Right to retain and instruct counsel without delay upon arrest or detention
charter_sections.s.11d: Right to be presumed innocent until proven guilty
charter_sections.s.15: Right to equality before and under the law, equal protection and benefit of the law
charter_sections.s.24: Remedies — exclusion of evidence or stay of proceedings for Charter violations
